primary_accounting_issue: Qualification et classification IFRS 9 d’un actif financier au regard du critère des flux de trésorerie contractuels constitués uniquement de principal et d’intérêts

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping governance, and selected IFRS 9 because it directly governs the classification of financial assets using the SPPI criterion, whereas IAS 32 only provides the broader definition of a financial asset.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: Low uncertainty, because the question is centered on the SPPI classification test and resulting measurement categories, which are directly governed by IFRS 9.

primary_authority.reason: This directly governs the SPPI assessment and the resulting classification outcomes for financial assets.

supporting_authority.reason: This supports the analysis by defining what a financial asset is before IFRS 9 classification is applied.

peripheral_authority.reason: This is topically related to acquisition-date classification but does not help identify peer approaches for the SPPI issue itself.
This discusses plan assets and employee benefits and is not relevant to identifying peer IFRS 9 classification approaches for SPPI assets.

treatment_families.family: Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held to collect contractual cash flows
Financial assets with contractual cash flows that are solely payments of principal and interest and held both to collect contractual cash flows and to sell
Financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss when not classified in the other IFRS 9 categories
Financial assets designated at fair value through profit or loss to eliminate or significantly reduce an accounting mismatch

approaches.label: Amortised cost
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats this as a distinct top-level classification model when the asset passes SPPI and is held in a hold-to-collect business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through other comprehensive income
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats this as a distinct top-level classification model when the asset passes SPPI and is held in a collect-and-sell business model.

approaches.label: Fair value through profit or loss
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats this as a distinct top-level classification model for financial assets not classified at amortised cost or at fair value through other comprehensive income, including designation under the fair value option.
